methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Silicon Box is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Sehat Sutardja that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (including Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, and DEEPX) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implying $380M. Peer-median triangulation across the 6 private comparables yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B; this wider dispersion reflects material stage and technology heterogeneity within the cohort. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across the three tickers) was not applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $323M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $690M. Stage-progression suggests the conservative end of this range, while peer medians anchor toward the upper half. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and product-market validation relative to earlier-stage peers commanding higher multiples, as well as the appropriate stage-transition multiple given competitive dynamics in frontier chip design.
